signals.headline: Forward guidance:**
signals.raw:
Forward guidance:**

The Policy Board indicated it expects inflation to rise into the "mid to upper two-percent range" this year[3], and economists warn that upside risks remain substantial as oil prices exceed $100 per barrel[4]. However, the confirmed decision to hold rates steady and the below-consensus March inflation print likely prevented a more dramatic market move.

signals.headline: Key market-moving factors:**
signals.raw:
Key market-moving factors:**

The inflation rise was driven primarily by **surging oil prices** linked to the Iran conflict, with petroleum products jumping 10.4% over the month[4]. The Bank of Korea raised its 2026 inflation outlook to 2.2% from an earlier projection of 2.1%[2], signaling expectations for continued upward pressure.

signals.headline: Offsetting factors limiting immediate market reaction:**
signals.raw:
Offsetting factors limiting immediate market reaction:**

Government measures—including fuel price caps implemented for the first time in nearly 30 years and food vouchers—successfully contained the March inflation reading below expectations[1][4]. Core inflation excluding food and energy edged down to 2.2%[1], suggesting the commodity price shock hasn't broadened to other sectors yet.
